inline_comments.message: I did not see it last time but we should not use snapshot version normally
will this still build if we remove "-SNAPSHOT", even though the maven artifacts have not been released yet for these new versions?

I need the snapshot versions of policy common and models because we are fixing a few issues in the Policy repos when we come across them and need to take the updates, especially in policy models.....There's even a review coming today once I finish the unit test.

The Tosca PoC code will build as of now without the snapshots but not if any more fixes are pushed so we'd like to stay on the snapshots for now....I think it's OK because it's all in the same project anyhow.

Ok but try to avoid that 😊
It's different repos/builds so it's not really the same project.
This leads to unstable/unreproductible builds 😊
I asked, because I've seen Seb strip off the "-SNAPSHOT" and have it still build.  If that's the case, then you could potentially use "1.8.1" & "2.4.1" and thus still get the updated versions.

@Seb, we normally build policy repos using snapshot versions so-as to always be up to date with the latest changes, even if it breaks something.  The advantage is that it identifies inconsistencies sooner.

Yes i have well understood the game 😊
In the context of an open source it may not be appropriate, your project "should" always build (in theory)
Previously in clamp it was not possible to do so, i think there was a plugin in the OParent to check that

Do you know if we disable it in the integration policy parent or ? It has been removed from the Oparent may be ?
No it is still there in oparent - it should have failed as part of the release job. Unless its defined in the <parent> tag. Which is not the case here.
